q_knee_region.label: Primary knee region
q_knee_region.prompt: Point to where your knee feels the worst.

test_decline_squat.title: Heel-elevated squat test
test_decline_squat.instructions: Place your heels on a small book or rolled towel and perform 3 slow squats. Report if the front of the knee feels better, same, or worse.

test_hamstring_bridge.title: Single-Leg Bridge Test
test_hamstring_bridge.instructions: Lift into a bridge on one leg. Stop if pain jumps above 5/10 and report how it felt.

q_symptom_duration.prompt: How long has this knee pain been around?

dx_patellofemoral.explanation: Elevated pain with decline squats suggests the kneecap joint is sensitive. Load shifts and gradual exposure usually help.

recommendations.title: Try an upright squat with a hold
recommendations.description: Keep the torso tall and squat only as deep as pain allows, pausing for a 5-second hold at the easiest depth.

dx_patellar_tendon.summary: Patellar tendon is tolerating heel lift
dx_patellar_tendon.explanation: Feeling better with heels elevated points toward patellar tendon overload that likes reduced compression.

recommendations.title: Decline isometric hold
recommendations.description: Hold a shallow decline squat for 30 seconds, 4-5 reps, keeping pain ≤ 3/10.

dx_posterior_chain.explanation: Pain with a single-leg bridge suggests the posterior capsule or hamstring insertion is sensitive right now.

recommendations.title: Bridge with hands-supported start
recommendations.description: Start the bridge with both legs, then unweight one leg at the top for 3-5 seconds to ease the load.

dx_chronic_overload.explanation: Symptoms beyond 12 weeks point to a slower adaptation curve. Consistency and low pain targets matter most.

recommendations.title: Build a small daily habit
recommendations.description: Do a 10-minute routine most days instead of sporadic long sessions.

dx_general_overload.explanation: No single structure dominates. We'll calm things down briefly and reload with controlled steps.

recommendations.title: Stick to walk/bike intervals
recommendations.description: Alternate 2 minutes easy walk with 1 minute relaxed cycling for 10-15 minutes to keep blood flow without flare-ups.
